activities_projects.title: Shoe Size Survey
activities_projects.objective: Understand how to collect and organize data
steps: Ask 10 friends or family members for their shoe size
Record the data in a raw list
Organize the data into a table from smallest to largest size
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns the importance of organizing raw data
activities_projects.evaluation: Clean and organized data table (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Finding the Average Height
steps: Measure the height of 5 students in centimeters
Add all the heights together
Divide the total sum by 5 to find the mean height
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the formula for Arithmetic Mean
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct calculation of the average (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Range of Daily Temperature
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the range of a data set
steps: Note the maximum temperature of your city for 7 days
Identify the highest and lowest temperatures
Subtract the lowest from the highest to find the Range
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how to find the spread of data
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of max, min, and range (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Favorite Fruit Mode
activities_projects.objective: Identify the Mode of a data set
steps: Survey 15 people about their favorite fruit (Apple, Mango, or Banana)
Count which fruit appears most frequently
Label this most frequent fruit as the 'Mode'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the most frequent value in a set
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the mode (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Median Middleman
activities_projects.objective: Find the Median of an odd number of data points
steps: Write 7 different numbers on cards and shuffle them
Arrange the cards in ascending order
Identify the card that sits exactly in the center
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the concept of the middle value
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the central value (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Tally Mark Practice
activities_projects.objective: Use tally marks for frequency counting
steps: Observe the colors of 20 passing cars or objects
Use tally marks (III I) to record the frequency of each color
Total the tally marks to find the frequency
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters the use of tally marks for data recording
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate tallying and total counts (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Simple Bar Graph
steps: Use the data from the 'Favorite Fruit' activity
Draw a horizontal axis for fruits and a vertical axis for the number of people
Draw bars of equal width for each fruit category
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Creates a visual representation of frequency
activities_projects.evaluation: Neatness and accuracy of the bar graph (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interpreting Daily Study Hours
steps: List your study hours for the last week
Find on which day you studied the most and the least
Calculate the average study time per day
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops the skill to draw conclusions from personal data
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct interpretation of the list (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Coin Toss Probability
steps: Toss a coin 10 times
Record the number of Heads and Tails
Discuss if it is 'certain' or 'likely' to get a head next
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Introduces the concept of chance and outcomes
activities_projects.evaluation: Record of outcomes and logical discussion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mean vs. Mode Comparison
steps: Write the numbers: 2, 2, 2, 10
Calculate the Mean and find the Mode
Discuss which number 'represents' the group better
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that mean and mode can be very different
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct calculation of both values (10 marks)
